description: SDF lens blur background canvas renders on page load using Three.js with OrthographicCamera
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify a <canvas> element exists in the DOM
Step 3: Verify the canvas is rendered by THREE.WebGLRenderer
Step 4: Verify the canvas uses position: fixed, top: 0, left: 0
Step 5: Verify the canvas sits at z-index 0 behind all UI elements
Step 6: Verify canvas width matches window.innerWidth
Step 7: Verify canvas height matches window.innerHeight
Step 8: Verify WebGL context is active (no context lost)
Step 9: Verify copper-tinted SDF shapes are visible on the canvas
Step 10: Verify UI content (wordmark, input) is visible above the canvas

description: SDF shader uses OrthographicCamera with correct bounds matching viewport dimensions
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Inspect Three.js scene — camera must be OrthographicCamera, not PerspectiveCamera
Step 3: Verify camera bounds: left=-w/2, right=w/2, top=h/2, bottom=-h/2
Step 4: Verify camera near=0.1, far=1000, position.z=1

description: SDF shader renders PlaneGeometry(1,1) scaled to viewport dimensions
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify a PlaneGeometry(1,1) mesh exists in the scene
Step 3: Verify quad.scale equals (viewportWidth, viewportHeight, 1)

description: SDF shader uses exactly three uniforms: u_mouse, u_resolution, u_pixelRatio
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Inspect ShaderMaterial uniforms object
Step 3: Verify u_mouse is vec2 type
Step 4: Verify u_mouse value is in page pixels (not normalized 0-1)
Step 5: Verify u_mouse points to damped vector, not raw mouse
Step 6: Verify u_resolution is vec2 type
Step 7: Verify u_resolution equals viewport dimensions * devicePixelRatio
Step 8: Verify u_pixelRatio is float type
Step 9: Verify u_pixelRatio is capped at 2 (Math.min(devicePixelRatio, 2))
Step 10: Verify no additional uniforms exist beyond these three

description: SDF shader uses VAR=0 define for round rectangle stroke variation
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify ShaderMaterial defines include VAR: 0
Step 3: Verify no URL parameter switching logic exists

description: SDF shader fragment.glsl is loaded as raw string import
steps: Step 1: Verify fragment.glsl exists at src/shaders/fragment.glsl
Step 2: Verify next.config.ts configures raw loader for .glsl files
Step 3: Verify SdfBackground.tsx imports the shader as a string

description: SDF shader vertex shader is inline (not a separate file)
steps: Step 1: Verify no vertex.glsl file exists in the project
Step 2: Verify vertex shader is defined inline in SdfBackground.tsx as a template string
Step 3: Verify vertex shader passes through position and sets v_texcoord = uv

description: SDF shader outputs copper-tinted color instead of monochrome white
steps: Step 1: Open fragment.glsl
Step 2: Verify the final color output uses copper grading (vec3 based on #D86A3D)
Step 3: Verify the original vec3(sdf) monochrome output has been replaced
Step 4: Navigate to landing page and visually confirm copper-tinted SDF shapes on dark background

description: SDF shader mouse tracking uses damped interpolation for smooth cursor following
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Move cursor quickly across the page
Step 3: Verify raw mouse position is captured via mousemove event listener
Step 4: Verify raw mouse position is also captured via pointermove event listener
Step 5: Verify raw mouse stored in vMouse (THREE.Vector2)
Step 6: Verify damped mouse stored in separate vMouseDamp (THREE.Vector2)
Step 7: Verify damping uses THREE.MathUtils.damp with lambda=8 and deltaTime
Step 8: Verify damping runs per-frame for both x and y independently
Step 9: Verify u_mouse uniform points to vMouseDamp reference, not vMouse
Step 10: Visually confirm smooth eased tracking of cursor in SDF shapes — no snapping

description: SDF shader animation loop runs at 60fps using requestAnimationFrame
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Open Chrome DevTools Performance tab
Step 3: Record 3 seconds of idle animation
Step 4: Verify frame rate is stable around 60fps
Step 5: Verify delta time is calculated from performance.now() * 0.001

description: SDF shader handles window resize correctly — updates camera, quad, resolution, pixelRatio
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page at 1920x1080
Step 2: Resize browser window to 1024x768
Step 3: Verify renderer.setSize called with new dimensions
Step 4: Verify camera bounds updated and updateProjectionMatrix called
Step 5: Verify quad.scale.set updated to new dimensions
Step 6: Verify u_resolution updated with new dimensions * dpr
Step 7: Verify u_pixelRatio updated
Step 8: Visually confirm shader fills viewport without distortion

description: SDF shader pixel ratio is capped at 2 for performance
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page on a high-DPI display (3x)
Step 2: Verify Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2) is used
Step 3: Verify renderer.setPixelRatio receives capped value

description: SDF shader touch events prevent scroll with passive:false on touchmove
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page on a touch device
Step 2: Touch and drag on the canvas area
Step 3: Verify body touchmove event has preventDefault called
Step 4: Verify event listener uses { passive: false }

description: SDF shader fragment.glsl contains correct SDF functions: sdRoundRect, sdCircle, sdPoly, aastep, fill, stroke
steps: Step 1: Open fragment.glsl
Step 2: Verify coord(vec2 p) function exists for coordinate normalization
Step 3: Verify sdRoundRect(vec2 p, vec2 b, float r) function exists
Step 4: Verify sdCircle(vec2 st, vec2 center) function exists
Step 5: Verify sdPoly(vec2 p, float w, int sides) function exists
Step 6: Verify aastep(float threshold, float value) function exists using fwidth
Step 7: Verify fill(float x) basic fill function exists
Step 8: Verify fill(float x, float size, float edge) overload exists
Step 9: Verify stroke(float d, float t) basic stroke function exists
Step 10: Verify stroke(float x, float size, float w, float edge) overload exists
Step 11: Verify main() function uses VAR preprocessor conditional for shape selection

description: SDF lens blur effect: cursor circle SDF modulates edge parameter of main shape
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Move cursor over the SDF shape
Step 3: Verify the shape edge softens/blurs near the cursor position
Step 4: Move cursor away from the shape — edge sharpens again
Step 5: Verify cursor circle SDF uses circleSize=0.3 and circleEdge=0.5 parameters
Step 6: Verify cursor circle fill value feeds into main shape stroke() edge parameter
Step 7: Verify this is edge parameter modulation, not a post-process blur filter
Step 8: Move cursor to different positions — verify effect follows smoothly
Step 9: Verify the effect works with VAR=0 round rectangle stroke variation
Step 10: Verify effect is visible on both desktop Chrome and Firefox

description: Landing page wordmark displays as 'Good'ai' with correct formatting
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify wordmark text reads 'Good'ai' (lowercase good, apostrophe, lowercase ai)
Step 3: Verify wordmark is NOT displayed as 'GoodAI', 'GOODAI', 'Good AI', or 'Good.ai'
Step 4: Verify apostrophe character renders in accent color (#D86A3D) at 0.7 opacity

description: Landing page subtitle displays 'business automations, sorted' in mono font
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify subtitle text reads 'business automations, sorted'
Step 3: Verify subtitle uses JetBrains Mono font
Step 4: Verify subtitle is uppercase

description: Landing page input field has placeholder 'Tell us your problem.'
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify input field is present and centered
Step 3: Verify placeholder text reads 'Tell us your problem.'
Step 4: Verify input has max-width 540px

description: Landing page hint text displays 'We'll figure out how to fix it.' below input
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify hint text below input reads 'We'll figure out how to fix it.'
Step 3: Verify hint uses JetBrains Mono font

description: Landing page Perth badge displays 'PERTH / WA' fixed at bottom center
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify Perth badge text reads 'PERTH / WA'
Step 3: Verify badge is fixed positioned at bottom center (28px from bottom)
Step 4: Verify slash character renders in accent-soft (#C4714D) color at 0.5 opacity
Step 5: Verify badge uses JetBrains Mono font-family
Step 6: Verify badge font-size is 10px
Step 7: Verify badge font-weight is 300
Step 8: Verify badge letter-spacing is 0.12em
Step 9: Verify badge text-transform is uppercase
Step 10: Verify badge color is text-dim (#6B6560)
Step 11: Verify badge fades up with 1.2s animation delay on load

description: Landing page submit button is an arrow icon inside the input field, right-aligned
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify submit button is positioned absolute inside input wrapper, right 8px
Step 3: Verify button is 40x40px with transparent background
Step 4: Verify button contains an SVG arrow icon (18px, stroke currentColor)

description: Landing page staggered fade-up animations play on load with correct delays
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page (hard refresh)
Step 2: Verify wordmark fades up with 0.3s delay, 1s duration
Step 3: Verify subtitle fades up with 0.55s delay
Step 4: Verify input area fades up with 0.7s delay
Step 5: Verify Perth badge fades up with 1.2s delay
Step 6: Verify all animations use cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1) easing

description: Input field focus state: border color changes, box-shadow appears, background elevates
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Inspect input field in default (unfocused) state
Step 3: Verify default border is 1px solid #2A2925
Step 4: Verify default background is #151514 (surface)
Step 5: Click on the input field to focus it
Step 6: Verify border-color changes to copper at 0.25 opacity
Step 7: Verify box-shadow appears with 4px accent-dim spread
Step 8: Verify box-shadow includes 32px dark blur component
Step 9: Verify background changes to surface-raised (#1C1C1A)
Step 10: Click away to blur the input
Step 11: Verify all focus styles revert to defaults smoothly

description: Submitting a message transitions from landing state to conversation state
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Type 'My invoicing takes forever' in the input field
Step 3: Press Enter or click the submit button
Step 4: Verify container shifts from center-justified to flex-start with padding-top 48px
Step 5: Verify wordmark shrinks from 34px to 20px, margin-bottom reduces to 28px
Step 6: Verify subtitle is hidden
Step 7: Verify landing input area is hidden
Step 8: Verify conversation div becomes visible
Step 9: Verify fixed bottom input bar appears
Step 10: Verify Perth badge is hidden
Step 11: Verify user message appears as right-aligned copper bubble

description: Conversation mode wordmark shrinks with smooth 0.5s ease transition
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Submit a message to trigger conversation mode
Step 3: Verify wordmark font-size transitions from 34px to 20px
Step 4: Verify transition duration is 0.5s with ease timing
Step 5: Verify margin-bottom transitions from 56px to 28px

description: User message bubble renders with correct styling — right-aligned, copper-tinted
steps: Step 1: Submit a message in the chat
Step 2: Verify message container has text-align: right
Step 3: Verify bubble has display: inline-block, max-width 85%
Step 4: Verify background is rgba(216,106,61,0.07)
Step 5: Verify border is 1px solid rgba(216,106,61,0.12)
Step 6: Verify border-radius is 16px 16px 4px 16px (bottom-right sharp)
Step 7: Verify padding is 14px 20px

description: AI message bubble renders with correct styling — left-aligned, surface background, GOOD'AI label
steps: Step 1: Submit a message and wait for AI response
Step 2: Verify AI message has 'GOOD'AI' label above the bubble
Step 3: Verify label uses JetBrains Mono, 10px, weight 400, letter-spacing 0.08em, uppercase
Step 4: Verify label color is accent-soft (#C4714D)
Step 5: Verify bubble background is surface (#151514)
Step 6: Verify bubble border is 1px solid border color (#2A2925)
Step 7: Verify border-radius is 16px 16px 16px 4px (bottom-left sharp)

description: Typing indicator shows three bouncing dots while waiting for AI response
steps: Step 1: Submit a message in the chat
Step 2: Verify typing indicator appears inside an AI message bubble with GOOD'AI label
Step 3: Verify three dot spans are rendered, each 5px x 5px, border-radius 50%
Step 4: Verify dots use accent-soft color
Step 5: Verify dots have staggered bounce animation (0s, 0.2s, 0.4s delay)
Step 6: Verify typing indicator disappears when AI response arrives

description: Input field is disabled and dimmed (opacity 0.5) while waiting for AI response
steps: Step 1: Submit a message in the chat
Step 2: Verify the fixed bottom input field becomes disabled
Step 3: Verify input opacity drops to 0.5
Step 4: Try typing — verify input does not accept text
Step 5: After AI response arrives, verify input re-enables and opacity returns to 1

description: Messages appear with fadeUp animation (translateY 10px, 0.5s duration)
steps: Step 1: Submit a message in the chat
Step 2: Verify new user message animates in with fadeUp (translateY 10px to 0)
Step 3: Wait for AI response
Step 4: Verify AI message also animates in with fadeUp

description: Conversation area auto-scrolls to bottom when new messages are added
steps: Step 1: Send several messages to fill the conversation area
Step 2: Verify conversation scrolls to the bottom after each new message
Step 3: Verify scrolling happens for both user messages and AI responses

description: Conversation area has custom scrollbar: 3px width, border color thumb, transparent track
steps: Step 1: Send enough messages to make conversation scrollable
Step 2: Verify scrollbar width is 3px
Step 3: Verify scrollbar thumb color matches border color (#2A2925)
Step 4: Verify scrollbar track is transparent

description: Chat history is stored in client-side array with { role, content } format
steps: Step 1: Send a message
Step 2: Wait for AI response
Step 3: Verify messageHistory array contains user message with role 'user'
Step 4: Verify messageHistory array contains AI response with role 'ai'
Step 5: Send another message and verify array grows correctly

description: Fixed bottom input bar appears only in conversation state with gradient background
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Verify no fixed bottom input bar is visible
Step 3: Submit a message to enter conversation mode
Step 4: Verify fixed bottom input bar appears at position: fixed, bottom: 0
Step 5: Verify background uses linear-gradient(transparent, var(--bg) 35%)
Step 6: Verify input wrapper has max-width 540px, centered

description: Fixed bottom input bar has correct padding: 20px 40px 32px
steps: Step 1: Enter conversation mode
Step 2: Verify fixed input bar padding is 20px top, 40px left/right, 32px bottom

description: Conversation div has padding-bottom 120px to prevent messages being hidden behind fixed input
steps: Step 1: Enter conversation mode
Step 2: Send several messages to fill the conversation area
Step 3: Verify conversation div has padding-bottom 120px
Step 4: Verify last message is visible above the fixed input bar

description: Both landing and fixed input forms handle submit via Enter key and button click
steps: Step 1: Navigate to landing page, type a message, press Enter
Step 2: Verify form submits and conversation mode activates
Step 3: In conversation mode, type a message in fixed input, press Enter
Step 4: Verify message submits
Step 5: Type another message and click the submit button
Step 6: Verify message submits via button click too

description: Empty message submission is prevented — nothing happens on empty submit
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Click submit or press Enter with empty input
Step 3: Verify no message is sent and no transition occurs

description: API route /api/chat exists as a Next.js Route Handler accepting POST requests
steps: Step 1: Send POST to /api/chat with { messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'My invoicing takes forever' }] }
Step 2: Verify response status is 200
Step 3: Verify response body contains { response: string }
Step 4: Verify response text is non-empty

description: API route uses Vercel AI Gateway via @ai-sdk/openai-compatible, not direct Anthropic SDK
steps: Step 1: Inspect /api/chat/route.ts source code
Step 2: Verify import of createOpenAICompatible from @ai-sdk/openai-compatible
Step 3: Verify baseURL is 'https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1'
Step 4: Verify model is gateway('anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514')
Step 5: Verify API key comes from process.env.AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY

description: API route maps frontend 'ai' role to 'assistant' role for API compatibility
steps: Step 1: Send POST to /api/chat with messages containing role 'ai'
Step 2: Verify the route handler maps 'ai' to 'assistant' before sending to AI Gateway
Step 3: Verify response is returned successfully

description: API route enforces max_tokens: 300 for concise responses
steps: Step 1: Inspect /api/chat/route.ts source code
Step 2: Verify max_tokens (or maxTokens) is set to 300
Step 3: Send a complex message and verify response is reasonably short

description: API route system prompt defines Good'ai intake assistant persona correctly
steps: Step 1: Inspect /api/chat/route.ts source code
Step 2: Verify system prompt establishes identity as Good'ai intake assistant in Perth, WA
Step 3: Verify system prompt uses 'we' and 'us'
Step 4: Verify system prompt instructs AI to never say 'AI', 'artificial intelligence', etc.
Step 5: Verify system prompt instructs AI to never use bullet points or numbered lists
Step 6: Verify system prompt instructs AI to keep responses under 3 sentences
Step 7: Verify system prompt mentions Perth SME context (tradies, 50s-60s owners)

description: AI response never contains the word 'AI' or 'artificial intelligence'
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'What kind of AI do you use?'
Step 2: Verify response does not contain 'AI', 'artificial intelligence', 'machine learning', or 'neural network'
Step 3: Verify response uses terms like 'automation', 'system', 'workflow' instead

description: AI response never uses corporate speak: no 'leverage', 'synergy', 'optimize', 'utilize', 'streamline'
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'How can you help my business?'
Step 2: Verify response does not contain 'leverage', 'synergy', 'optimize', 'utilize', or 'streamline'
Step 3: Verify tone is casual and direct

description: AI response never uses bullet points or numbered lists
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'What services do you offer?'
Step 2: Verify response does not contain bullet points (-, *, •) or numbered lists (1., 2.)
Step 3: Verify response uses natural sentences instead

description: AI response is under 3 sentences for simple queries
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'I spend too long on invoicing'
Step 2: Count sentences in the response
Step 3: Verify response is 3 sentences or fewer

description: AI first response acknowledges the problem, gives insight, asks one clarifying question
steps: Step 1: Send first message: 'We waste hours manually entering data from emails'
Step 2: Verify response acknowledges the problem
Step 3: Verify response includes a useful insight about automation
Step 4: Verify response ends with or includes one clarifying question

description: AI recognizes Perth SME tools: Xero, MYOB, ServiceM8, Tradify, Cliniko, Square
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'We use Xero for accounting but it takes ages'
Step 2: Verify response acknowledges familiarity with Xero
Step 3: Send follow-up: 'We also use ServiceM8 for job management'
Step 4: Verify response acknowledges ServiceM8

description: AI gently redirects off-topic questions back to business automations
steps: Step 1: Send message: 'What's the weather like in Perth today?'
Step 2: Verify response gently redirects to business automations
Step 3: Verify response is not rude or dismissive

description: API route returns friendly message on 429 rate limit error
steps: Step 1: Trigger a 429 rate limit error from the AI Gateway
Step 2: Verify response message is: 'We're getting a lot of traffic right now. Give it a sec and try again.'
Step 3: Verify no technical error details are exposed

description: API route returns friendly message on 500 server error
steps: Step 1: Trigger a 500 server error
Step 2: Verify response message is: 'Something broke on our end. Try again in a moment.'
Step 3: Verify no stack traces are exposed

description: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is server-side only — never exposed to the browser
steps: Step 1: Open browser DevTools Network tab
Step 2: Submit a chat message
Step 3: Inspect all requests — verify AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY does not appear in any request headers, body, or response
Step 4: Inspect page source — verify no API key in client-side JavaScript

description: Lead capture card appears 600ms after first AI response
steps: Step 1: Submit first message in the chat
Step 2: Wait for AI response to render
Step 3: Verify lead capture card appears approximately 600ms after the AI response
Step 4: Verify card is injected into the conversation flow (not a separate overlay)

description: Lead capture card heading reads 'Want us to look into this?'
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Verify heading text is 'Want us to look into this?'
Step 3: Verify heading is 15px, weight 600, text-bright color

description: Lead capture card subtext reads 'Drop your details and we'll scope it out — no obligation, no runaround.'
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Verify subtext matches exactly
Step 3: Verify subtext is 13px, text-dim color, line-height 1.5

description: Lead capture card has four fields in two rows: Name+Business, Phone+Email
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Verify Row 1 contains Name (required) and Business name (optional) fields
Step 3: Verify Row 2 contains Phone (required, type=tel) and Email (optional) fields
Step 4: Verify each row has two fields side by side (flex row, gap 10px)

description: Lead capture Name field is required
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Leave Name field empty, fill Phone
Step 3: Click submit
Step 4: Verify form validation prevents submission

description: Lead capture Phone field is required and uses type=tel
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Verify Phone input has type='tel'
Step 3: Fill Name but leave Phone empty
Step 4: Click submit
Step 5: Verify form validation prevents submission

description: Lead capture Business and Email fields are optional
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Fill only Name and Phone (required fields)
Step 3: Leave Business and Email empty
Step 4: Click submit
Step 5: Verify form submits successfully

description: Lead capture submit button text is 'Get a callback'
steps: Step 1: Trigger lead capture card
Step 2: Verify submit button text is 'Get a callback'

description: Lead capture submit button changes to 'Sending...' and disables during submission
steps: Step 1: Fill in lead capture form fields
Step 2: Click 'Get a callback'
Step 3: Verify button text changes to 'Sending...'
Step 4: Verify button becomes disabled (opacity 0.5, no pointer events)

description: Lead capture form submits to Web3Forms API with correct payload
steps: Step 1: Open DevTools Network tab
Step 2: Fill in lead capture form: Name='John', Business='Acme', Phone='0412345678', Email='john@acme.com'
Step 3: Click submit
Step 4: Verify POST request to https://api.web3forms.com/submit
Step 5: Verify payload contains access_key (WEB3FORMS_KEY)
Step 6: Verify payload contains subject 'New lead: John'
Step 7: Verify payload contains from_name "Good'ai Website"
Step 8: Verify payload contains name, business, phone, email fields
Step 9: Verify payload contains 'problem' field with first user message
Step 10: Verify payload contains 'conversation' field with full message history formatted as 'Them: ... / Us: ...'

description: Lead capture success state shows checkmark and confirmation message
steps: Step 1: Submit the lead capture form
Step 2: Verify card inner HTML replaces with success state
Step 3: Verify checkmark icon (28px, accent color) is displayed
Step 4: Verify text reads 'Nice one. We'll be in touch within 24 hours.'
Step 5: Verify success state is text-align center with 20px padding

description: Lead capture shows success state even on Web3Forms error (never lose the lead)
steps: Step 1: Block network request to web3forms.com (simulate error)
Step 2: Fill and submit the lead capture form
Step 3: Verify success state still shows (checkmark + confirmation text)

description: Lead capture card only shows once per session — leadCaptured flag prevents re-showing
steps: Step 1: Submit first message, wait for AI response
Step 2: Verify lead capture card appears after 600ms
Step 3: Fill in Name and Phone fields
Step 4: Click 'Get a callback' to submit
Step 5: Verify success state replaces form
Step 6: Verify leadCaptured flag is set to true
Step 7: Send a second chat message
Step 8: Wait for second AI response
Step 9: Verify no second lead capture card appears
Step 10: Send a third chat message and wait for response
Step 11: Verify still no additional lead card — flag persists for session

description: User can continue chatting normally after submitting the lead form
steps: Step 1: Complete the lead capture flow (submit form, see success)
Step 2: Type a new message in the fixed input
Step 3: Verify message sends and appears in conversation
Step 4: Verify AI responds normally
Step 5: Verify no errors or UI glitches

description: Subsequent messages append below the lead card in the conversation flow
steps: Step 1: Complete the lead capture flow
Step 2: Send a new message
Step 3: Verify the new message appears BELOW the lead card in the DOM
Step 4: Verify conversation order is: user msg, AI msg, lead card, user msg 2, AI msg 2

description: Custom cursor renders as 20px circle with 1.5px copper border
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page on desktop
Step 2: Verify custom cursor div exists in the DOM
Step 3: Verify position: fixed on cursor element
Step 4: Verify z-index: 9999 on cursor element
Step 5: Verify pointer-events: none on cursor element
Step 6: Verify width is 20px and height is 20px
Step 7: Verify border-radius is 50%
Step 8: Verify border is 1.5px solid rgba(216,106,61,0.35)
Step 9: Verify mix-blend-mode: difference is applied
Step 10: Verify transform uses translate(-50%, -50%) for centering on cursor position

description: Custom cursor expands to 48px on hover over interactive elements (inputs, buttons, links)
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Hover cursor over the input field
Step 3: Verify cursor expands to 48px x 48px
Step 4: Verify border color brightens to rgba(216,106,61,0.55)
Step 5: Move cursor away from input
Step 6: Verify cursor returns to 20px default

description: Custom cursor follows mouse with smooth lerp (0.12 factor) via requestAnimationFrame
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page
Step 2: Move mouse quickly across the screen
Step 3: Verify cursor follows with smooth lag (not snapping instantly to mouse position)
Step 4: Verify animation uses requestAnimationFrame loop with 0.12 lerp factor

description: Custom cursor hover listeners re-register when new DOM elements are injected
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page, verify cursor expands on landing input hover
Step 2: Verify cursor expands on landing submit button hover
Step 3: Submit a message to enter conversation mode
Step 4: Hover over the new fixed bottom input — verify cursor expands to 48px
Step 5: Hover over the new fixed submit button — verify cursor expands
Step 6: Wait for lead capture card to appear
Step 7: Hover over lead card Name input — verify cursor expands
Step 8: Hover over lead card Business input — verify cursor expands
Step 9: Hover over lead card Phone input — verify cursor expands
Step 10: Hover over lead card Email input — verify cursor expands
Step 11: Hover over lead card 'Get a callback' button — verify cursor expands
Step 12: Move cursor away from all elements — verify cursor returns to 20px default

description: Custom cursor is completely hidden on touch devices
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page on a touch device (or emulate touch in DevTools)
Step 2: Verify touch detection uses 'ontouchstart' in window check
Step 3: Verify custom cursor div is hidden (display: none or visibility: hidden)
Step 4: Verify body cursor CSS is set to 'auto' (native cursor visible)
Step 5: Verify ambient glow div is also hidden
Step 6: Verify no cursor-related requestAnimationFrame loop is running
Step 7: Verify input fields do NOT have cursor: none on touch devices
Step 8: Verify page is fully functional without custom cursor
Step 9: Verify touch interactions (tap, scroll) work normally
Step 10: Verify no hover expansion effects are attempted on touch

description: Ambient glow renders as 700px radial gradient following cursor
steps: Step 1: Navigate to the landing page on desktop
Step 2: Verify ambient glow div exists in the DOM
Step 3: Verify position: fixed on glow element
Step 4: Verify z-index: 0 on glow element
Step 5: Verify pointer-events: none on glow element
Step 6: Verify width is 700px and height is 700px
Step 7: Verify border-radius is 50%
Step 8: Verify background uses radial-gradient(circle, rgba(216,106,61,0.04) 0%, transparent 65%)
Step 9: Verify transition uses 0.9s cubic-bezier(0.23, 1, 0.32, 1)
Step 10: Move mouse — verify glow repositions with transform translate(mouseX-350, mouseY-350)

description: Ambient glow is hidden on touch devices
steps: Step 1: Navigate to landing page on a touch device

description: Noise texture overlay renders via body::before pseudo-element
